title: Evangelion Highest Grossing Franchise in History
display_title: Evangelion Highest Grossing Franchise in History
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion: Highest Grossing Franchise in History             Quote Post by Gendo777 » Sun May 19, 2024 7:37 pm With only 26 episodes, 6 movies, 6 mangas, and 7 video games, the franchise has made $8 billion , not counting merchandise and pachinko slot sales, making it the highest grossing franchise per-episode, per-movie, per-manga volume, and per-game. The only franchises that exceed Evangelion in terms of non-merchandise sales are Pokemon, Star Wars, Mario, Harry Potter, Marvel Universe, DC Universe, Dragon Ball, Fist of the North Star, One Piece, Lord of the Rings, and James Bond, which still do not count considering the sheer number of books, comics, music, movies, TV series, and video games that needed to be made just for these franchises to reach those numbers. Even the Marvel and DC universe can be completely discounted because they are comprised of multiple superhero franchises. Evangelion only had a few production runs, but it made that much money. When the $9 billion in merchandise and pachinko slot sales is added, it has made $17 billion . Sources: https://www.cbr.com/highest-grossing-anime-franchises/ https://www.titlemax.com/discovery-cent ... -all-time/ The series got 100% on Rotten Tomatoes: https://www.rottentomatoes.com/tv/neon_ ... evangelion The End of Evangelion got 92% on Rotten Tomatoes: https://www.rottentomatoes.com/m/the_end_of_evangelion The latest movie: Evangelion: 3.0+1.01 Thrice Upon a Time also got 100% on Rotten Tomatoes: https://www.rottentomatoes.com/m/evange ... pon_a_time

[]: Re: Evangelion: Highest Grossing Franchise in History             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Mon May 20, 2024 1:56 am While you're not generally wrong, much of this data, including for other franchises, is outdated. I remember Eva was listed in the highest-grossing franchises article on Wikipedia some years ago ahead of (or right behind?) The Simpsons (lol), but now it's far behind at 2 billion USD. Some of those numbers are weird, it seems to me Japanese Yen has often not been converted...

[]: Re: Evangelion: Highest Grossing Franchise in History             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon May 20, 2024 7:12 am FelipeFritschF wrote: While you're not generally wrong, much of this data, including for other franchises, is outdated. I remember Eva was listed in the highest-grossing franchises article on Wikipedia some years ago ahead of (or right behind?) The Simpsons (lol), but now it's far behind at 2 billion USD. Some of those numbers are weird, it seems to me Japanese Yen has often not been converted...

[]: Re: Evangelion: Highest Grossing Franchise in History             Quote Post by Gendo777 » Mon May 20, 2024 9:27 am FelipeFritschF wrote: While you're not generally wrong, much of this data, including for other franchises, is outdated. I remember Eva was listed in the highest-grossing franchises article on Wikipedia some years ago ahead of (or right behind?) The Simpsons (lol), but now it's far behind at 2 billion USD. Some of those numbers are weird, it seems to me Japanese Yen has often not been converted...

[]: Re: Evangelion: Highest Grossing Franchise in History             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu May 23, 2024 11:37 pm It is very impressive indeed. Although, as Felipe pointed out, I always found those numbers weird. It's just hard to believe. But I guess Japan really loves Evangelion and its merch. I would argue that it is the most popular anime production that didn't start with a manga (that would probably be Dragon Ball, followed by One Piece, maybe). I think one could argue that Gundam is more popular, but I personally don't think so. But again, it's crazy to believe that EVA (according to these numbers) made more money than Sonic, Donkey Kong, Naruto etc.
